data.text:
• The cell is the basic unit of life. All living organisms are composed of one or more cells.
• Cells arise from pre-existing cells through cell division.
• The cell membrane encloses the cytoplasm and regulates what enters and exits the cell.
Key concepts: membrane, organelle, cytoskeleton, nucleus, mitochondria, ribosome

rows: A lipid bilayer that encloses the cell and controls transport
Powerhouse of the cell; produces ATP via cellular respiration
Molecular machine that synthesises proteins from mRNA
Membrane-bound organelle that contains genetic material

data.prompt: Ask for a concept map of Chapter 1
